purpose: Falsification test: is the reported ~4 s / ~100 in bout scale a biological run timescale or a segmentation artifact?
units: inches (WISER native, UNVERIFIED offset frame); seconds

data.source: cached cleaned night positions (re-extracted from positions, NOT route_bouts.csv)

verdict: D (segmentation-defined scale) for the reported '~4 s capacity / ~8 ft length'; true structure is B (ballistic reorientation-punctuated primitives, near-memoryless hazard) + C (fragments of paddock-scale multi-stop trips). A (genuine characteristic run duration) is FALSIFIED.

coverage.A7_pauses_decision: partial (turn-angle across pause vs within run; full landmark/social A7 deferred)
coverage.A10_distributions: done (exp/gamma/weibull/lognormal + piecewise hazard; power-law not fit - durations bounded)

caveats: scipy/statsmodels/sklearn absent -> MLE, hazard, model comparison implemented in numpy; truncated-gamma normalization approximated
durations left-censored at ~2 samples (~0.13-0.4 s) even at min_bout=0
inch frame UNVERIFIED; speeds/distances are internal
stationary-noise sigma for A8 estimated ~5 in/axis (fallback if baseline unreadable)
